Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 12:32 ET
Dispersion trades emerge as single-name call demand surges against index put protection
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Institutional dispersion setup crystallizing with index P/C at 1.65 (puts) while single-names at 0.60 (calls) — widest divergence since Aug selloff
  • 2. Defense/aerospace names explode higher on call volume with RTX +1008% and BA +675% as sector rotation accelerates into earnings
  • 3. Six tech names in backwardation signal event risk ahead of TSLA/MSFT/META earnings clustered around GDP print
NARRATIVE — dispersion setup bullish backdrop

The most striking development is the emergence of a textbook institutional dispersion trade, with index puts surging to 1.65 P/C while single-name calls dominate at 0.60 P/C — the widest divergence since the August unwind. This structure, combined with implied correlation collapsing to 0.04, signals hedge funds are betting on stock-specific outcomes while maintaining portfolio hedges into the GDP/earnings gauntlet. Defense names are witnessing explosive call demand as rotation momentum builds, with RTX call volume spiking +1008% and BA calls up +675% despite the latter also seeing heavy put flow (+707%). The two-way BA flow reflects uncertainty around its earnings Thursday, while RTX's pure call bias suggests conviction on the sector's trajectory. Positive dealer gamma at $+4.3B for SPY creates a technical backdrop that should contain broad market moves while amplifying single-name dispersions. Tech vol structure reveals mounting event anxiety, with AMD, ARM, and TSLA all in backwardation — a rare condition where near-term IV exceeds forward vol. This setup, concentrated in Thursday's TSLA print and next week's MSFT/META cluster, suggests options markets are pricing binary outcomes rather than gradual moves. The 0.04 implied correlation reading reinforces that stock-specific catalysts will drive performance over macro themes through month-end.

Layer 1: Sentiment +50Layer 2: GEX +$4.3BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.65/0.60Layer 4: IC 0.04 stock-specific

TSLA options in backwardation with near-term IV elevated ahead of Thursday earnings, signaling market expects binary outcome rather than gradual moves. Technical death cross conflicts with vol demand, creating unusual risk/reward asymmetry. No recent news catalyst — purely earnings-driven positioning.
